title: Automate External Data Ingestion
description: Automate EPI and WGI dataset ingestion to eliminate manual CSV uploads. EU Supply Shares already automated. Research phase complete with feasible automation paths identified.
acceptanceCriteria: EPI data source URL identified and documented
WGI data source URL identified and documented
Automated ingestion working for both datasets (HTTP or API)
Pipeline runs end-to-end without manual file upload
dependencies: Network access from Fabric to external URLs
No firewall restrictions blocking HTTP downloads
notes: Research phase COMPLETE - Both datasets can be fully automated
- Created fabric/bronze_ingest_epi.Notebook/ (downloads EPI CSV from Yale, writes to bronze_epi2024results)
- Created fabric/bronze_ingest_wgi.Notebook/ (calls World Bank API v2 for 6 WGI indicators, writes to bronze_WGI)
- Both notebooks follow Fabric notebook format (CELL/METADATA/MARKDOWN markers, lakehouse metadata)
- Schema-compatible with downstream bronze-to-silver notebook (verified column names)

SELF-REVIEW: Verified notebook format matches existing notebooks, column names match downstream expectations (bronze-to-silver), error handling and retry logic included, pipeline JSON intentionally NOT modified (Erik does pipeline wiring in Fabric UI)
- Replace bronze_EPI/bronze_WGI RefreshDataflow activities with TridentNotebook activities in pipeline
- Decommission old EPI_file2table and WGI_file2table dataflows

checks: WGI source URL documented: https://api.worldbank.org/v2/ (source=3) — PASS

external_data_automation.md updated with implementation details — PASS

task_verification.notes: All acceptance criteria met for the code-deliverable portion. Pipeline wiring (subtasks 3-4) require Erik in Fabric UI. Cannot run notebooks locally — runtime verification requires Fabric environment.
